paper.title: An Extremely Massive White Dwarf Escaped From the Hyades Star Cluster

review.summary: Single-table paper. Table 1 (tab:wd_derived) contains 27 data columns across 3 sub-tables for WD1: Gaia EDR3 astrometry/photometry (9 cols), Pan-STARRS1+GALEX photometry (7 cols), derived+spectroscopic quantities (11 cols). No machine-readable supplementary tables provided. External resources: 7 URLs (Gaia archive, pyphot, Astropy, MAST, Gaia ESA, DPAC). Key external datasets referenced: Gaia EDR3, Fusillo WD catalog, SDSS, GALEX, Pan-STARRS1, Gemini GMOS spectroscopy, PARSEC isochrones, Bedard+2020 CO cooling models, Althaus+2022 ONe cooling models, Tremblay+2011 DA atmospheric models, Caiazzo+2021 mass-radius relations.

source_refs.caption: WD1 astrometric and photometric quantities from Gaia EDR3 (top row), additional photometry from Galex and Pan-STARRS1 (middle row), along with derived and spectroscopic quantities (bottom row). Spectroscopic estimates for radius, mass and age obtained with the assumption of an ONe core composition.
source_refs.label: tab:wd_derived

columns.name: Gaia DR3 Source ID
columns.meaning: Unique Gaia DR3 source identifier for WD1

columns.name: RA
columns.meaning: Right Ascension of WD1 from Gaia EDR3

columns.name: Dec
columns.meaning: Declination of WD1 from Gaia EDR3

columns.name: Parallax
columns.meaning: Gaia EDR3 parallax measurement

columns.name: pm RA
columns.meaning: Proper motion in Right Ascension from Gaia EDR3

columns.name: pm Dec
columns.meaning: Proper motion in Declination from Gaia EDR3

columns.name: G
columns.meaning: Gaia G-band apparent magnitude

columns.name: Abs G
columns.meaning: Absolute G-band magnitude derived from parallax

columns.name: Bp-Rp
columns.meaning: Gaia BP-RP color index

columns.name: PS1-y
columns.meaning: Pan-STARRS1 y-band apparent magnitude

columns.name: PS1-z
columns.meaning: Pan-STARRS1 z-band apparent magnitude

columns.name: PS1-i
columns.meaning: Pan-STARRS1 i-band apparent magnitude

columns.name: PS1-r
columns.meaning: Pan-STARRS1 r-band apparent magnitude

columns.name: PS1-g
columns.meaning: Pan-STARRS1 g-band apparent magnitude

columns.name: Galex-NUV
columns.meaning: GALEX near-ultraviolet apparent magnitude

columns.name: Galex-FUV
columns.meaning: GALEX far-ultraviolet apparent magnitude

columns.name: d_present
columns.meaning: Present-day heliocentric distance of WD1

columns.name: v_r
columns.meaning: Reconstructed radial velocity from escapee trajectory analysis

columns.name: d_min
columns.meaning: Minimum distance between WD1 and cluster center at closest approach

columns.name: Δv_3D
columns.meaning: Reconstructed 3D velocity relative to cluster center

columns.name: t_escape
columns.meaning: Time since closest approach to cluster center (escape time)

columns.name: log g
columns.meaning: Surface gravity from spectroscopic Balmer line fitting

columns.name: T_eff
columns.meaning: Effective temperature from spectroscopic fitting

columns.name: v_z,g
columns.meaning: Gravitational redshift velocity (corrected for radial motion)

columns.name: R
columns.meaning: WD1 radius from ONe core cooling models

columns.name: M
columns.meaning: WD1 mass from ONe core cooling models

columns.name: t_cool
columns.meaning: WD1 cooling age from ONe core cooling models

internal_tables.evidence: WD1 astrometric and photometric quantities from Gaia EDR3 (top row), additional photometry from Galex and Pan-STARRS1 (middle row), along with derived and spectroscopic quantities (bottom row). Spectroscopic estimates for radius, mass and age obtained with the assumption of an ONe core composition.
internal_tables.comments: Single-row table with one data row for WD1. Top sub-table: Gaia DR3 Source ID=560883558756079616, RA=39.6517 deg, Dec=76.7052 deg, Parallax=5.98 mas, pmRA=46.46 mas/yr, pmDec=-26.49 mas/yr, G=19.21 mag, Abs G=13.09 mag, Bp-Rp=-0.40 mag. Middle sub-table: PS1 photometry (y,z,i,r,g) and GALEX (NUV,FUV). Bottom sub-table: d_present=167 pc, v_r=20.4 km/s, d_min=21.38 pc, Δv_3D=9.98 km/s, t_escape=15 Myr, log g=9.55±0.11, T_eff=26400±200 K, v_z,g=260±21 km/s, R=2230+280/-250 km, M=1.317+0.014/-0.018 M_⊙, t_cool=556+15/-22 Myr.

external_resources.description: Gaia archive; used to access gaiaedr3.dr2_neighborhood crossmatch routine for crossmatching Gaia DR2 Hyades catalog with EDR3

source_refs.context: Using this sample, we crossmatch with the Gaia EDR3 catalogue using the routine gaiaedr3.dr2_neighborhood (available at the Gaia archive)

external_resources.evidence: Footnote in Section 2.1 (Current Cluster): crossmatch routine gaiaedr3.dr2_neighborhood available at Gaia archive
external_resources.comments: Essential for building the Hyades cluster member sample

external_resources.description: pyphot package; used to compute synthetic photometry from normalized synthetic spectra for photometric radius fit

source_refs.context: we compute synthetic photometry using the pyphot package

external_resources.evidence: Footnote in Section 2.4 (Theoretical Mass-Radius Relation): synthetic photometry computed using pyphot package

external_resources.description: Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST); GALEX data archive

source_refs.context: The Pan-STARRS1 Surveys (PS1) and the PS1 public science archive...

external_resources.comments: GALEX photometry used for WD1 photometric radius determination

external_resources.description: ESA Gaia mission homepage; data from Gaia DR2 and EDR3 used throughout the paper
